title: symphytum officinale subsp. bohemicum
intro: Symphytum officinale subsp. bohemicum, also known as common comfrey or Bohemian comfrey, is a subspecies of the Symphytum officinale plant, a perennial herb belonging to the borage family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antitussive effects, making it a popular remedy for wound healing, joint pain, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Symphytum officinale subsp. bohemicum include promoting tissue repair, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting the respiratory system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, creams, and salves, and are often used topically to accelerate wound healing and reduce inflammation.
